title: Add curl installer
description:
## Why
README advertised manual clone/install only. Users wanted a one-command installer like curl -fsSL ... | bash.

## Done When
- scripts/install.sh clones or fast-forwards pro-cli safely.
- README documents quick, custom-path, inspect-first, and manual install paths.
- pro --version verifies linked CLI installs.
close_reason: Implemented in 8deb624. Added scripts/install.sh, README quick/custom/inspect/manual install docs, pro --version support, and CLI coverage. Verified with bash -n scripts/install.sh, bun test, tsc --noEmit, and bun src/cli.ts --version.

title: Epic: Surface ChatGPT web response metadata
description:
## Why
The ChatGPT web stream carries useful structured data that pro-cli currently discards: reasoning progress, resolved model metadata, continuation ids, limits, and resume hints. Exposing curated pieces would make pro-cli more transparent and agent-friendly without dumping sensitive raw stream data.

## Scope
Design and implement a curated metadata layer for ChatGPT web responses, plus commands that use it. Keep raw cookies, tokens, profile metadata, and internal ids redacted unless explicitly needed for debugging.

## Done When
- Response metadata extraction is documented and tested.
- Reasoning progress can surface during waits.
- Successful calls return continuation ids.
- Limits are available in a JSON command.
- Discovery tooling exists for future endpoint/request-shape research.
- README and docs/chatgpt-web-api-handbook.md stay aligned.

title: Extract curated ChatGPT response metadata
description:
## Why
pro-cli currently treats the ChatGPT web stream mostly as answer text. The stream also carries continuation ids, resolved model state, reasoning progress, limits, and tool metadata that downstream commands need.

## Done When
- The response parser returns answer text plus a typed metadata object.
- Metadata includes conversationId, assistant/input message ids, parent id, request/turn ids, resolved/default model slugs, reasoning markers, limits, and tool/search markers when present.
- Raw auth, resume tokens, profile text, and other sensitive fields are redacted or omitted by default.
- Tests cover snapshot and patch-style SSE fixtures, including missing-field cases.

title: Show live Pro reasoning progress while waiting
description:
## Why
ChatGPT Pro exposes reasoning and progress markers during long jobs. Agents and humans should see useful live status instead of guessing whether a wait timeout means stalled, still thinking, or nearly done.

## Done When
- The daemon persists latest reasoning/progress metadata for each running job.
- `pro-cli job wait`, `pro-cli job create --wait`, and any interactive/human output can show concise progress without spamming.
- JSON output includes stable progress fields suitable for agents.
- Timeout responses include the latest observed progress and next suggested command.
- Tests cover progress updates, reconnects, and jobs with no progress metadata.

title: Return continuation handles from successful results
description:
## Why
The web API returns enough ids to continue a conversation, but pro-cli does not yet expose them clearly. Continuation should be explicit, scriptable, and compatible with temporary conversations.

## Done When
- Successful `ask`, `job result`, and waited job responses return conversationId, assistantMessageId, parentMessageId/nextParentId, and request id when available.
- Continuation flags are documented with clear temporary-versus-saved conversation behavior.
- Continuing a conversation uses the same metadata fields, not scraped text or hidden state.
- Tests cover continuation extraction and follow-up request shape construction.

title: Add a pro-cli limits command
description:
## Why
The web stream and model metadata can expose usage limits and reset hints. A command that surfaces those hints would help agents choose wait/retry behavior and avoid blind failed calls.

## Done When
- `pro-cli limits --json` returns curated limit information from the latest live metadata and/or model endpoints when available.
- Human output is concise and distinguishes known limits from unavailable/unknown values.
- `doctor` points users to `limits` when auth is working but usage may be constrained.
- Missing or changed web fields degrade to a clear unavailable state.
- Tests cover known, unknown, and partial limit payloads.

title: Add redacted ChatGPT web API capture tooling
description:
## Why
Future endpoint research should be repeatable and safe. Today exploration uses one-off scripts in /private/tmp, which makes it easy to lose useful probes or accidentally collect too much raw session data.

## Done When
- A developer/debug command captures selected ChatGPT web request and response shapes through the existing authenticated browser session.
- The capture path redacts auth headers, cookies, resume tokens, account/profile fields, prompt bodies by default, and other sensitive values.
- Output can be written to /private/tmp or an explicit path outside source control.
- The command supports small request-shape experiments through explicit options.
- docs/chatgpt-web-api-handbook.md explains the workflow and safety rules.

title: Explore tool search and Deep Research request shapes
description:
## Why
Search, tool calls, file uploads, and Deep Research likely use related but distinct request fields and stream metadata. We should map those paths before promising first-class support.

## Done When
- CDP traces for normal search, Deep Research, and at least one tool/file-assisted flow are captured with the redacted tooling.
- The handbook summarizes endpoints, request fields, stream events, metadata fields, and observed failure modes.
- Stable fields are converted into implementation beads or added to the metadata extraction issue.
- Unstable or unclear fields are explicitly marked as provisional.
